title: Bringing Robots to Life with Vivid Descriptions

questions.question: What does "tremulous" mean?
options: The name of the new robot character in the story.
Something that is known to be true or proved.
To figure something out using reasoning and clues from the text, rather than it being directly stated.
Shaking slightly, often from nervousness or excitement.
questions.correct_answer: Shaking slightly, often from nervousness or excitement.
questions.explanation: "tremulous" means: Shaking slightly, often from nervousness or excitement.

options: To figure something out using reasoning and clues from the text, rather than it being directly stated.

questions.correct_answer: The name of the new robot character in the story.
questions.explanation: "EGR3" means: The name of the new robot character in the story.

questions.question: Fill in the blank: Use precise words to ______ characters and settings.
questions.explanation: The full sentence is: "Use precise words to describe characters and settings."

questions.question: Which of the following is TRUE about this topic?
options: Practice descriptive writing by imagining your own robot.
Always proofread for spelling and legibility.
Differentiate between facts (stated directly) and inferences (concluded from clues).
Use precise words to describe characters and settings.
questions.correct_answer: Differentiate between facts (stated directly) and inferences (concluded from clues).
questions.explanation: Correct! "Differentiate between facts (stated directly) and inferences (concluded from clues)." is one of the key things to remember.

options: To figure something out using reasoning and clues from the text, rather than it being directly stated.
Shaking slightly, often from nervousness or excitement.
The name of the new robot character in the story.
Something that is known to be true or proved.
questions.correct_answer: Something that is known to be true or proved.
questions.explanation: "fact" means: Something that is known to be true or proved.
